input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a CollectionView with single selection and pull-to-refresh:

**XAML:**

```xml
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.ItemsPage">

    <RefreshView IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
                 Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}">

        <CollectionView ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
                        SelectionMode="Single"
                        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
                        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">
            <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
                <DataTemplate>
                    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="10">
                        <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="18" />
                        <Label Text="{Binding Description}" FontSize="14" TextColor="Gray" />
                    </VerticalStackLayout>
                </DataTemplate>
            </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
        </CollectionView>

    </RefreshView>
</ContentPage>
```

**ViewModel:**

```csharp
public partial class ItemsViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ItemModel _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<ItemModel> _items = new();

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task Refresh()
    {
        Items = new ObservableCollection<ItemModel>(await _api.GetItemsAsync());
        IsRefreshing = false;
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task SelectionChanged()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.NavigateToAsync(nameof(DetailPage), 
            new Dictionary<string, object> { { "Item", SelectedItem } });

        SelectedItem = null; // reset so user can re-select
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| `IsRefreshing` | Controls the refresh spinner; set `false` when done |
| `RefreshCommand` | Fires on pull-to-refresh gesture |
| `ItemsSource` | The collection of data items |
| `SelectedItem` | Two-way binding; holds the tapped item |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | Fires when selection changes; triggers navigation |

**Tips:**
- Set `SelectedItem = null` after navigating so the same item can be tapped again.
- `IsRefreshing = false` must be set explicitly to dismiss the spinner.
- Uses MVVM Toolkit (`[ObservableProperty]`, `[RelayCommand]`) to reduce boilerplate.
